name: Pickled Dilled Beans
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 8 pints
Procedure:
1. Wash and trim ends from beans and cut to 4-inch lengths.
2. In each sterile pint jar, place 1-2 dill heads and, if desired, 1 clove of garlic.
3. Place whole beans upright in jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
4. Trim beans to ensure proper fit, if necessary.
5. Combine salt, vinegar, water, and pepper flakes (if desired).
6. Bring mixture to a boil.
7. Add hot solution to beans, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
8. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Pickled Dilled Beans in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Pints.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 min
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 10 min
Above 6,000 ft: 15 min
